text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. Each day begins with a standup meeting, where team members quickly share what they did yesterday, what they'll do today, and any problems. The team's tasks are kept in a backlog, a prioritized list of work. This backlog is made up of user stories, which describe features from a user's perspective. Through iterative development, teams build and improve the product step by step, getting feedback after each sprint to make sure they meet user needs efficiently.
